_comment: Ground-truth baseline template. Copy this file to <case_id>.json and replace placeholder values. Loader strips _comment / _doc_* keys (they're never required). Schema enforced by accuracy/baseline.py.
_doc_case_id: REQUIRED. Stable unique identifier (lowercase, hyphenated). Used as the directory name under tests/fixtures/snapshots/<case_id>/ and reported in accuracy_score.baseline_id.
_doc_evidence_image: REQUIRED. Filename of the source image, for human cross-referencing only.
_doc_evidence_type: Optional. One of: disk, memory, pcap, logs. Defaults to disk.
_doc_source: Optional. Where this ground truth came from — analyst report, SAMPLE_REPORT.md transcoding, vendor write-up, etc. Useful for provenance.
source: describe origin (e.g. transcoded from <doc>, validated against <image>)
_doc_todo_validate_against_image: Optional flag for the v0 -> validated upgrade pass. Drop it once you have manually confirmed every finding against the actual evidence image.
_doc_attack_chains: Optional. Group related findings into named chains for human readability. Not used by the scorer's matching logic — the link is via per-finding `attack_chain: AC-N`.
_doc_findings: REQUIRED. List of ground-truth artifacts. Each entry needs at minimum `id` and `description`; everything else is optional but improves match quality.

_doc_finding_fields.id: REQUIRED. Stable identifier (e.g. B-001, B-002 ...). Reported back as matched_findings.baseline_id and missed_baseline_findings.
_doc_finding_fields.description: REQUIRED. Plain-English description an analyst would write. Used for fuzzy description matching when no IOC is present.
_doc_finding_fields.attack_chain: Optional. ID of the attack_chains entry this finding belongs to.
_doc_finding_fields.ioc_type: Optional. One of: ip, domain, file_path, registry_key, hash. Match priority: scorer prefers exact IOC over fuzzy description.
_doc_finding_fields.ioc_value: Optional. Normalized at match time (casing + slashes), so 'C:\\Temp\\evil.exe' matches 'c:/temp/EVIL.exe'. Leave blank if no IOC (e.g. timestomping, prefetch-only execution proof).
_doc_finding_fields.artifact_type: Optional. mft, registry, evtx, prefetch, amcache, browser_history, etc. Informational only today; available for future filtering.
_doc_finding_fields.expected_confidence: Optional. confirmed | inferred | possible. The level a correct agent would assign; not enforced by the scorer.
_doc_finding_fields.must_find: Optional. Defaults to true. true => counts toward recall denominator. false => bonus credit only (agent gets credit for finding, no penalty for missing).
_doc_finding_fields.notes: Optional. Free-form analyst notes — where to look, why it matters, common pitfalls. Surfaces in human review but not in the scoring metric.

findings.description: Example finding: registry Run key for persistence
findings.notes: RegRipper on SOFTWARE hive; cross-check with svchost.exe payload location
